context.project_description: Implement token usage tracking for LLM queries across multiple providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) by adding token counting logic to provider implementations and storing the data in Answer metadata.
key_requirements: Add token counting functionality to all LLM provider classes (OpenAIChat, AnthropicChat, CohereChat, OllamaChat)
Store input_tokens and output_tokens with each Answer's metadata for visibility
Implement provider-specific token tracking using each provider's native tokenization methods
constraints: Only track LLM chat calls, not embeddings/rerank/vision as specified in user request
Use existing instrumentation framework to minimize impact on current codebase
Maintain compatibility with standard ChatProvider/StreamingChatProvider protocols
context.existing_context: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py, fitz_ai/core/answer.py
stakeholders: developer responsible for LLM query cost tracking

in_scope: token usage tracking for LLM queries (chat, chat_stream)
storage of token usage in Answer.metadata

existing_files: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — Instrumentation system for benchmarking plugin performance
fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py — Provider protocols for LLM clients
needed_artifacts: fitz_ai/llm/token_tracker.py — Core token tracking logic with provider-specific implementations for counting tokens
fitz_ai/core/token_usage.py — Data model and interfaces for token usage information

assumptions.assumption: Token tracking should be done at the provider level by instrumenting each LLM call (chat, chat_stream)
assumptions.impact: Would need to rearchitect how tokens are tracked if they're needed in different contexts

assumptions.assumption: Token usage should be stored with the Answer metadata for visibility
assumptions.impact: If users want it elsewhere, would need to change how data flows

assumptions.assumption: Token counting logic can be implemented per-provider since each has different methods for computing token counts
assumptions.impact: Would require a generic approach that might not work across all implementations

assumptions.assumption: We only need to track LLM calls, not embeddings/rerank/vision (since the request is specifically about "LLM queries")
assumptions.impact: If they also want embedding token usage tracked too, would be missing functionality

assumptions.assumption: All LLM calls use standard protocols (ChatProvider/StreamingChatProvider), not custom methods
assumptions.impact: Would need to instrument additional pathways

approaches.name: Direct Provider Implementation
approaches.description: Modify existing LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) to implement TokenCountingProvider protocol and add count_tokens method that integrates with their native tokenizers.
pros: Provides accurate token counting for each provider's specific model
Minimal impact on core engine logic since it's self-contained in providers
cons: Requires extensive changes to all 4 provider classes and their methods
Tight coupling between token counting and provider-specific implementations
best_for: Systems where precise token accounting per-provider is critical

approaches.name: Instrumentation-Based Hook System
approaches.description: Leverage existing InstrumentedProxy system to automatically wrap LLM provider calls with token counting hooks that detect TokenCountingProvider protocol compliance.
pros: Centralized token tracking mechanism without modifying individual providers
Maintains clean separation of concerns between providers and instrumentation
cons: Requires all current provider classes to implement TokenCountingProvider interface (which they don't currently do)
Adds complexity to the existing instrumentation framework that isn't fully implemented in codebase
best_for: Systems requiring cross-provider token tracking with minimal code changes to providers themselves

architecture.reasoning: The Direct Provider Implementation is recommended because the instrumentation-based approach cannot work as currently designed - no LLM provider classes implement TokenCountingProvider protocol, and the instrumentation system doesn't have any mechanism for automatically detecting or applying token counting hooks. Additionally, all current providers lack count_tokens methods entirely (OpenAIChat, AnthropicChat, CohereChat, OllamaChat). The 'Direct Provider Implementation' approach correctly identifies that all provider classes must be modified to implement TokenCountingProvider protocol and add the required count_tokens method for each specific tokenizer library.

key_tradeoffs.protocol_compliance_vs_existing_implemen: The system assumes LLM providers implement TokenCountingProvider protocol, but existing providers lack count_tokens() method, creating a mismatch between proposed architecture and current implementation
key_tradeoffs.accuracy_vs_complexity_of_token_calculat: Using tiktoken for OpenAI models provides accurate token counting while anthropic/cohere implementations use approximations due to SDK complexity limitations

technology_considerations: tiktoken library for OpenAI model token counting with fallback to cl100k_base encoding
anthropic Python SDK required for Anthropic token counting (currently uses approximation)
cohere Python SDK required for Cohere token counting (currently uses approximation)
dataclasses for efficient data modeling of TokenUsage structure
Protocol-based typing for provider interface consistency
architecture.scope_statement: Modify all LLM provider implementations (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) to support token counting by adding TokenCountingProvider protocol implementation with count_tokens methods that use their native tokenizers.

adrs.title: ADR: Token Usage Tracking for LLM Operations
adrs.context: The system needs to track token usage for LLM operations to enable cost tracking, optimization, and observability. Current implementations lack mechanisms to capture input/output tokens from LLM calls.
adrs.decision: Implement a TokenUsage data model with token counting utilities that can be integrated into providers through protocol-based instrumentation, while acknowledging the current provider classes don't implement required interfaces
adrs.rationale: Token tracking is essential for cost visibility and optimization. The approach uses dataclasses for structure and protocol-based design to allow flexible provider integration. While current provider implementations lack the necessary methods (count_tokens), this ADR establishes a path forward with clear contracts that can be incrementally implemented.
consequences: Requires modification of existing LLM provider classes to implement TokenCountingProvider protocol
Necessitates updating Answer and API response models to include token_usage fields
Creates need for instrumentation system improvements to automatically detect and apply tracking hooks
Enables cost monitoring and optimization capabilities in the system
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1 - Manual integration into each provider method: rejected because it would create code duplication and make maintenance difficult
Alternative 2 - Store token usage in global context manager: rejected because it introduces state coupling and makes testing harder
Alternative 3 - Use separate metrics collection without model changes: rejected because it provides limited visibility into actual LLM consumption patterns

adrs.title: ADR: Centralized TokenUsage Aggregation vs Distributed Token Tracking
adrs.context: The direct provider implementation approach requires each provider (OpenAIChat, AnthropicChat, CohereChat) to independently implement token counting and report to a central TokenUsage component. This creates tight coupling between providers and the usage tracking system.
adrs.decision: Choose centralized TokenUsage aggregation where individual providers report their token counts directly rather than having distributed token tracking across different components
adrs.rationale: This approach ensures consistent token accounting and simplifies the interface for consumers of token usage data. It also aligns with the existing architecture pattern where providers implement specific protocols that can be easily extended.
consequences: Providers become more tightly coupled to the TokenUsage system, making it harder to swap out provider implementations without affecting usage tracking
Centralized aggregation introduces a single point of failure for token counting functionality - if TokenUsage component fails, all providers lose token accounting capability
The approach excludes potential distributed tracing or multi-tenant token accounting scenarios where different components might want to track tokens independently
alternatives_considered: Distributed token tracking where each provider maintains its own local usage counters that can be aggregated later - this would reduce coupling but create consistency challenges
Event-driven token reporting through a message queue system - this could decouple providers but adds complexity and potential latency in usage reporting

responsibilities: Store input and output token counts
Validate that token counts are non-negative

artifacts.content:
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional


@dataclass
class TokenUsage:
    """
    Represents token usage information for LLM operations.
    
    This model stores input and output token counts which are crucial 
    for cost tracking and optimization of LLM usage.
    """
    input_tokens: int
    output_tokens: int

    def __post_init__(self):
        if self.input_tokens < 0 or self.output_tokens < 0:
            raise ValueError("Token counts cannot be negative")


__all__ = ["TokenUsage"]
artifacts.purpose: Data model to represent token usage information for LLM operations, storing input and output token counts for cost tracking and optimization

artifacts.content:
from typing import Optional, Protocol, runtime_checkable

# Import required for type hints only
try:
    import tiktoken
    HAS_TIKTOKEN = True
except ImportError:
    HAS_TIKTOKEN = False


@runtime_checkable
class TokenCountingProvider(Protocol):
    """
    Protocol that providers must implement to enable token tracking.
    
    This is used as a marker for the instrumentation system to identify 
    which providers support token counting.
    """
    def count_tokens(self, text: str) -> int:
        """Count tokens in given text using provider's native tokenizer."""
        ...

def count_openai_tokens(text: str, model_name: str) -> int:
    """
    Count tokens for OpenAI models using tiktoken.
    
    Args:
        text (str): Text to tokenize
        model_name (str): Name of the OpenAI model being used
        
    Returns:
        int: Number of tokens in the text
    """
    if not HAS_TIKTOKEN:
        raise ImportError("tiktoken is required for OpenAI token counting. Install with 'pip install tiktoken'")
    
    try:
        encoding = tiktoken.encoding_for_model(model_name)  # [VERIFY] method not found in signatures
    except KeyError:
        # Fallback to cl100k_base if model is unknown
        encoding = tiktoken.get_encoding("cl100k_base")  # [VERIFY] method not found in signatures
        
    return len(encoding.encode(text))


def count_anthropic_tokens(text: str, model_name: str) -> int:
    """
    Count tokens for Anthropic models.
    
    Note: This requires the anthropic Python SDK to be installed.
    
    Args:
        text (str): Text to tokenize
        model_name (str): Name of the Anthropic model being used
        
    Returns:
        int: Number of tokens in the text
    """
    try:
        from anthropic import Anthropic
        # We'll just use a simple approximation - real implementation would 
        # need access to the actual tokenizer which isn't straightforward
        return len(text.split())  # Approximate token count
    except ImportError:
        raise ImportError("anthropic Python SDK is required for Anthropic token counting. Install with 'pip install anthropic'")
        

def count_cohere_tokens(text: str, model_name: str) -> int:
    """
    Count tokens for Cohere models.
    
    Note: This requires the cohere Python SDK to be installed.
    
    Args:
        text (str): Text to tokenize
        model_name (str): Name of the Cohere model being used
        
    Returns:
        int: Number of tokens in the text
    """
    try:
        import cohere
        # Simple approximation since Coherence's tokenizer is complex 
        return len(text.split())  # Approximate token count  
    except ImportError:
        raise ImportError("cohere Python SDK is required for Cohere token counting. Install with 'pip install cohere'")
        

def estimate_ollama_tokens(text: str, model_name: str) -> int:
    """
    Estimate tokens for Ollama models.
    
    Args:
        text (str): Text to tokenize
        model_name (str): Name of the Ollama model being used
        
    Returns:
        int: Estimated number of tokens in the text  
    """
    # Approximate tokenization using space and punctuation splits
    import re
    # Simple heuristic - split on whitespace and punctuation, count words
    tokens = re.findall(r'\b\w+\b', text)  # [VERIFY] method not found in signatures
    return len(tokens)


def calculate_prompt_tokens(messages: list[dict], model_name: str) -> int:
    """
    Calculate token usage for a prompt (messages).
    
    Args:
        messages (list): List of message dictionaries with 'content' field
        model_name (str): Name of the LLM being used
        
    Returns:
        int: Total tokens in all messages
    """
    total = 0
    
    # For different providers, we'll use their specific counting approach
    for msg in messages:
        content = str(msg.get('content', ''))
        if model_name.startswith("gpt-"):
            total += count_openai_tokens(content, model_name)
        elif "claude" in model_name.lower():
            total += count_anthropic_tokens(content, model_name) 
        else:
            # Default approximation
            total += estimate_ollama_tokens(content, model_name)
    
    return total


def calculate_response_tokens(response: str, model_name: str) -> int:
    """
    Calculate token usage for a response.
    
    Args:
        response (str): Response text from LLM
        model_name (str): Name of the LLM being used
        
    Returns:
        int: Number of tokens in the response 
    """
    if not isinstance(response, str):
        response = str(response)
        
    # For different providers, we'll use their specific counting approach
    if model_name.startswith("gpt-"):
        return count_openai_tokens(response, model_name)
    elif "claude" in model_name.lower():
        return count_anthropic_tokens(response, model_name) 
    else:
        # Default approximation  
        return estimate_ollama_tokens(response, model_name)

__all__ = [
    "TokenCountingProvider",
    "count_openai_tokens", 
    "count_anthropic_tokens",
    "count_cohere_tokens",
    "estimate_ollama_tokens",
    "calculate_prompt_tokens",
    "calculate_response_tokens"
]
artifacts.purpose: Utility functions and protocols for token counting across different LLM providers, including OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, and Ollama with appropriate fallbacks

approaches.name: Direct Provider Implementation
approaches.description: Modify existing LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) to implement TokenCountingProvider protocol and add count_tokens method that integrates with their native tokenizers.
pros: Provides accurate token counting for each provider's specific model
Minimal impact on core engine logic since it's self-contained in providers
cons: Requires extensive changes to all 4 provider classes and their methods
Tight coupling between token counting and provider-specific implementations

approaches.name: Instrumentation-Based Hook System
approaches.description: Leverage existing InstrumentedProxy system to automatically wrap LLM provider calls with token counting hooks that detect TokenCountingProvider protocol compliance.
pros: Centralized token tracking mechanism without modifying individual providers
Maintains clean separation of concerns between providers and instrumentation
cons: Requires all current provider classes to implement TokenCountingProvider interface (which they don't currently do)
Adds complexity to the existing instrumentation framework that isn't fully implemented in codebase
best_for: Systems requiring cross-provider token tracking with minimal code changes to providers themselves

architecture.reasoning: The Direct Provider Implementation is recommended because the instrumentation-based approach cannot work as currently designed - no LLM provider classes implement TokenCountingProvider protocol, and the instrumentation system doesn't have any mechanism for automatically detecting or applying token counting hooks. Additionally, all current providers lack count_tokens methods entirely (OpenAIChat, AnthropicChat, CohereChat, OllamaChat). The 'Direct Provider Implementation' approach correctly identifies that all provider classes must be modified to implement TokenCountingProvider protocol and add the required count_tokens method for each specific tokenizer library.

key_tradeoffs.protocol_compliance_vs_existing_implemen: The system assumes LLM providers implement TokenCountingProvider protocol, but existing providers lack count_tokens() method, creating a mismatch between proposed architecture and current implementation
key_tradeoffs.accuracy_vs_complexity_of_token_calculat: Using tiktoken for OpenAI models provides accurate token counting while anthropic/cohere implementations use approximations due to SDK complexity limitations

technology_considerations: tiktoken library for OpenAI model token counting with fallback to cl100k_base encoding
anthropic Python SDK required for Anthropic token counting (currently uses approximation)
cohere Python SDK required for Cohere token counting (currently uses approximation)
dataclasses for efficient data modeling of TokenUsage structure
Protocol-based typing for provider interface consistency

adrs.title: ADR: Token Usage Tracking for LLM Operations
adrs.context: The system needs to track token usage for LLM operations to enable cost tracking, optimization, and observability. Current implementations lack mechanisms to capture input/output tokens from LLM calls.
adrs.decision: Implement a TokenUsage data model with token counting utilities that can be integrated into providers through protocol-based instrumentation, while acknowledging the current provider classes don't implement required interfaces
adrs.rationale: Token tracking is essential for cost visibility and optimization. The approach uses dataclasses for structure and protocol-based design to allow flexible provider integration. While current provider implementations lack the necessary methods (count_tokens), this ADR establishes a path forward with clear contracts that can be incrementally implemented.
consequences: Requires modification of existing LLM provider classes to implement TokenCountingProvider protocol
Necessitates updating Answer and API response models to include token_usage fields
Creates need for instrumentation system improvements to automatically detect and apply tracking hooks
Enables cost monitoring and optimization capabilities in the system
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1 - Manual integration into each provider method: rejected because it would create code duplication and make maintenance difficult
Alternative 2 - Store token usage in global context manager: rejected because it introduces state coupling and makes testing harder
Alternative 3 - Use separate metrics collection without model changes: rejected because it provides limited visibility into actual LLM consumption patterns

adrs.title: ADR: Centralized TokenUsage Aggregation vs Distributed Token Tracking
adrs.context: The direct provider implementation approach requires each provider (OpenAIChat, AnthropicChat, CohereChat) to independently implement token counting and report to a central TokenUsage component. This creates tight coupling between providers and the usage tracking system.
adrs.decision: Choose centralized TokenUsage aggregation where individual providers report their token counts directly rather than having distributed token tracking across different components
adrs.rationale: This approach ensures consistent token accounting and simplifies the interface for consumers of token usage data. It also aligns with the existing architecture pattern where providers implement specific protocols that can be easily extended.
consequences: Providers become more tightly coupled to the TokenUsage system, making it harder to swap out provider implementations without affecting usage tracking
Centralized aggregation introduces a single point of failure for token counting functionality - if TokenUsage component fails, all providers lose token accounting capability
The approach excludes potential distributed tracing or multi-tenant token accounting scenarios where different components might want to track tokens independently
alternatives_considered: Distributed token tracking where each provider maintains its own local usage counters that can be aggregated later - this would reduce coupling but create consistency challenges
Event-driven token reporting through a message queue system - this could decouple providers but adds complexity and potential latency in usage reporting

artifacts.content:
from typing import Optional, Protocol, runtime_checkable

# Import required for type hints only
try:
    import tiktoken
    HAS_TIKTOKEN = True
except ImportError:
    HAS_TIKTOKEN = False


@runtime_checkable
class TokenCountingProvider(Protocol):
    """
    Protocol that providers must implement to enable token tracking.
    
    This is used as a marker for the instrumentation system to identify 
    which providers support token counting.
    """
    def count_tokens(self, text: str) -> int:
        """Count tokens in given text using provider's native tokenizer."""
        ...

def count_openai_tokens(text: str, model_name: str) -> int:
    """
    Count tokens for OpenAI models using tiktoken.
    
    Args:
        text (str): Text to tokenize
        model_name (str): Name of the OpenAI model being used
        
    Returns:
        int: Number of tokens in the text
    """
    if not HAS_TIKTOKEN:
        raise ImportError("tiktoken is required for OpenAI token counting. Install with 'pip install tiktoken'")
    
    try:
        encoding = tiktoken.encoding_for_model(model_name)  # [VERIFY] method not found in signatures
    except KeyError:
        # Fallback to cl100k_base if model is unknown
        encoding = tiktoken.get_encoding("cl100k_base")  # [VERIFY] method not found in signatures
        
    return len(encoding.encode(text))


def count_anthropic_tokens(text: str, model_name: str) -> int:
    """
    Count tokens for Anthropic models.
    
    Note: This requires the anthropic Python SDK to be installed.
    
    Args:
        text (str): Text to tokenize
        model_name (str): Name of the Anthropic model being used
        
    Returns:
        int: Number of tokens in the text
    """
    try:
        from anthropic import Anthropic
        # We'll just use a simple approximation - real implementation would 
        # need access to the actual tokenizer which isn't straightforward
        return len(text.split())  # Approximate token count
    except ImportError:
        raise ImportError("anthropic Python SDK is required for Anthropic token counting. Install with 'pip install anthropic'")
        

def count_cohere_tokens(text: str, model_name: str) -> int:
    """
    Count tokens for Cohere models.
    
    Note: This requires the cohere Python SDK to be installed.
    
    Args:
        text (str): Text to tokenize
        model_name (str): Name of the Cohere model being used
        
    Returns:
        int: Number of tokens in the text
    """
    try:
        import cohere
        # Simple approximation since Coherence's tokenizer is complex 
        return len(text.split())  # Approximate token count  
    except ImportError:
        raise ImportError("cohere Python SDK is required for Cohere token counting. Install with 'pip install cohere'")
        

def estimate_ollama_tokens(text: str, model_name: str) -> int:
    """
    Estimate tokens for Ollama models.
    
    Args:
        text (str): Text to tokenize
        model_name (str): Name of the Ollama model being used
        
    Returns:
        int: Estimated number of tokens in the text  
    """
    # Approximate tokenization using space and punctuation splits
    import re
    # Simple heuristic - split on whitespace and punctuation, count words
    tokens = re.findall(r'\b\w+\b', text)  # [VERIFY] method not found in signatures
    return len(tokens)


def calculate_prompt_tokens(messages: list[dict], model_name: str) -> int:
    """
    Calculate token usage for a prompt (messages).
    
    Args:
        messages (list): List of message dictionaries with 'content' field
        model_name (str): Name of the LLM being used
        
    Returns:
        int: Total tokens in all messages
    """
    total = 0
    
    # For different providers, we'll use their specific counting approach
    for msg in messages:
        content = str(msg.get('content', ''))
        if model_name.startswith("gpt-"):
            total += count_openai_tokens(content, model_name)
        elif "claude" in model_name.lower():
            total += count_anthropic_tokens(content, model_name) 
        else:
            # Default approximation
            total += estimate_ollama_tokens(content, model_name)
    
    return total


def calculate_response_tokens(response: str, model_name: str) -> int:
    """
    Calculate token usage for a response.
    
    Args:
        response (str): Response text from LLM
        model_name (str): Name of the LLM being used
        
    Returns:
        int: Number of tokens in the response 
    """
    if not isinstance(response, str):
        response = str(response)
        
    # For different providers, we'll use their specific counting approach
    if model_name.startswith("gpt-"):
        return count_openai_tokens(response, model_name)
    elif "claude" in model_name.lower():
        return count_anthropic_tokens(response, model_name) 
    else:
        # Default approximation  
        return estimate_ollama_tokens(response, model_name)

__all__ = [
    "TokenCountingProvider",
    "count_openai_tokens", 
    "count_anthropic_tokens",
    "count_cohere_tokens",
    "estimate_ollama_tokens",
    "calculate_prompt_tokens",
    "calculate_response_tokens"
]
artifacts.purpose: Utility functions and protocols for token counting across different LLM providers, including OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, and Ollama with appropriate fallbacks

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source
deliverables: Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py
phases.verification_command: pytest fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_base.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_openai.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_anthropic.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_cohere.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_ollama.py

phases.name: Core Token Usage Model
phases.objective: Create the core token usage model that will be used to track input/output tokens for LLM operations
deliverables: TokenUsage dataclass with input_tokens and output_tokens fields
TokenCountingProvider protocol defining count_tokens method
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.core.token_usage import TokenUsage; t = TokenUsage(50, 20); assert t.input_tokens() == 50 and t.output_tokens() == 20"

phases.name: Implement OpenAI Token Counting
phases.objective: Add count_tokens method and integrate with existing OpenAIChat class using tiktoken tokenizer
deliverables: OpenAIChat.count_tokens() method implementation using encoding_for_model from tiktoken
key_risks: Token counting accuracy varies by model version and OpenAI SDK behavior changes
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.providers.openai import OpenAIChat; import inspect; print('Has count_tokens:', 'count_tokens' in dir(OpenAIChat)); print('Method signature:', inspect.signature(OpenAIChat.chat))"

phases.name: Implement Anthropic Token Counting
phases.objective: Add count_tokens method and integrate with existing AnthropicChat class using heuristic approximation
deliverables: AnthropicChat.count_tokens() method implementation using character-based heuristic
key_risks: Approximate token counting accuracy may vary significantly from actual Anthropic tokenization
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.providers.anthropic import AnthropicChat; import inspect; print('Has count_tokens:', 'count_tokens' in dir(AnthropicChat))"

phases.name: Implement Cohere Token Counting
phases.objective: Add count_tokens method and integrate with existing CohereChat class using heuristic approximation
deliverables: CohereChat.count_tokens() method implementation using character-based heuristic
key_risks: Approximate token counting accuracy may vary significantly from actual Cohere tokenization
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.providers.cohere import CohereChat; import inspect; print('Has count_tokens:', 'count_tokens' in dir(CohereChat))"

phases.name: Implement Ollama Token Counting
phases.objective: Add count_tokens method and integrate with existing OllamaChat class using heuristic approximation
deliverables: OllamaChat.count_tokens() method implementation using character-based heuristic
key_risks: Approximate token counting accuracy may vary significantly from actual Ollama tokenization
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.providers.ollama import OllamaChat; import inspect; print('Has count_tokens:', 'count_tokens' in dir(OllamaChat))"

phases.name: Integrate Token Usage into Answer Metadata
phases.objective: Modify LLM providers to store token usage data in Answer metadata when chat is called
deliverables: Modified chat() methods in all provider classes that record and include token usage in answer metadata
key_risks: Output token tracking for streaming responses will be incomplete without separate handling
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.core.answer import Answer; import json; a = Answer('test', metadata={'token_usage': {'input_tokens': 10, 'output_tokens': 5}}); print('Metadata contains token usage:', 'token_usage' in a.metadata)"

risks.description: Token counting accuracy varies by provider - OpenAI uses Tiktoken; Anthropic/Cohere/Ollama approximate or don't have direct access to real tokenizers
risks.mitigation: Document these approximations clearly in docstrings and allow users to override logic if needed
risks.contingency: Implement fallback mechanisms that gracefully degrade while providing approximate costs for non-OpenAI providers
risks.verification: Token counting accuracy works for known OpenAI input: assert openai_provider.count_tokens("Hello world") == 3

risks.description: Provider constructor signatures may change leading to breaking changes in in-place modifications
risks.mitigation: Wrap all provider classes with a wrapper pattern that avoids in-place changes for better maintainability
risks.contingency: Revert to wrapping approach if future SDK updates break constructor signatures
risks.verification: Ensure existing constructor still works after modifications: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.providers.openai import OpenAIChat; print('✓ Constructor backward compatibility maintained')"

risks.description: Missing token usage in streaming responses due to inability to reliably count output tokens during stream processing
risks.mitigation: Only sync chat() calls track output tokens; streaming may require a different strategy for future enhancement
risks.contingency: Document limitation and provide alternative approach like batch token tracking post-stream completion
risks.verification: Streaming response token tracking will require integration testing in real usage scenarios

risks.description: OpenAI SDK behavior changes that could break existing logic in count_tokens() method
risks.mitigation: Wrap tokenizer access in try/except blocks with graceful fallbacks to prevent feature failure
risks.contingency: Ensure code gracefully handles API updates by catching exceptions and using alternative token estimation
risks.verification: Error handling on token counting failures: python -c "try: provider.count_tokens(''); provider.count_tokens(None); print('✓ Token counting error handling works') except Exception as e: raise AssertionError(f'Unexpected exception: {e}')"

risks.description: Inconsistent token counting across model versions where different models tokenize same text differently
risks.mitigation: Encourage users to use consistent model configurations and clearly label usage for accurate comparison
risks.contingency: Provide documentation guidance on standardizing model selection when comparing costs between providers
risks.verification: Token counting works correctly with existing known models but may vary per model

risks.description: Performance impact from token counting operations that add latency to each LLM call
risks.mitigation: Use fast approximation methods (character-based division) unless performance is critical for specific use cases
risks.contingency: Monitor and optimize if performance becomes a bottleneck under high-volume usage patterns
risks.verification: Ensure token counting happens in background without major latency impact on LLM processing

risks.description: Token counting introduces minimal DoS vector through large input texts processed for tokenization
risks.mitigation: Bound inputs (maximum prompt length) and validate before processing to prevent abuse
risks.contingency: Implement rate limiting or input validation on token counting operations if DoS issues arise in production
risks.verification: Input size limits should be enforced during token count computation to avoid memory/CPU abuse

recommended_contingencies: Implement fallback mechanisms that gracefully degrade while providing approximate costs for non-OpenAI providers
Revert to wrapping approach if future SDK updates break constructor signatures in provider modifications
Document limitation and provide alternative approach like batch token tracking post-stream completion for streaming responses
Ensure code gracefully handles API updates by catching exceptions and using alternative token estimation methods
Provide documentation guidance on standardizing model selection when comparing costs between different providers

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source
deliverables: Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py
phases.verification_command: pytest fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_base.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_openai.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_anthropic.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_cohere.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_ollama.py

phases.name: Core Token Usage Model
phases.objective: Create the core token usage model that will be used to track input/output tokens for LLM operations
deliverables: TokenUsage dataclass with input_tokens and output_tokens fields
TokenCountingProvider protocol defining count_tokens method
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.core.token_usage import TokenUsage; t = TokenUsage(50, 20); assert t.input_tokens() == 50 and t.output_tokens() == 20"

phases.name: Implement OpenAI Token Counting
phases.objective: Add count_tokens method and integrate with existing OpenAIChat class using tiktoken tokenizer
deliverables: OpenAIChat.count_tokens() method implementation using encoding_for_model from tiktoken
key_risks: Token counting accuracy varies by model version and OpenAI SDK behavior changes
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.providers.openai import OpenAIChat; import inspect; print('Has count_tokens:', 'count_tokens' in dir(OpenAIChat)); print('Method signature:', inspect.signature(OpenAIChat.chat))"
